marked2: She «sneezed» the napkin off the table.
She «sneezed» twice during the lecture.
He «coughed» the crumb across the table.
He «coughed» quietly into his sleeve.
She «blew» the dust off the shelf.
She «blew» gently on her hot tea.
He «puffed» the feather off his palm.
He «puffed» slowly on his pipe.
She «huffed» the wrapper off the tray.
She «huffed» in obvious frustration.
He «fanned» the smoke out of the room.
He «fanned» himself in the summer heat.
She «whistled» her way down the hall.
She «whistled» a cheerful little tune.
He «sang» his way through the crowd.
He «sang» a quiet evening lullaby.
She «danced» her way across the stage.
She «danced» gracefully all evening long.
He «joked» his way out of trouble.
He «joked» with his old friends.
She «smiled» her way into the job.
She «smiled» warmly at the arriving guests.
